body:
{
  "title": "Jasper Moreau — VP Engineering, Anchor Codec",
  "compiled_truth": "Jasper Moreau is VP Engineering at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), where he oversees the core engineering organization and owns the hiring pipeline for all technical roles. He joined in September 2027, roughly eighteen months into the company's life, at a point when the team was feeling the growing pains of shipping deterministic data infrastructure with a small but intense crew.

Before Anchor, Jasper held engineering leadership positions at two infrastructure companies — both through their Series B and C stages. At his most recent role he scaled the engineering team from 8 to 40 over about two years, which is the kind of growth trajectory that breaks most orgs. He's known for being unusually deliberate about team topology: he doesn't just hire fast, he hires in a sequence that avoids the typical dysfunction of rapid scaling. People who've worked with him describe a guy who thinks about org design the way most engineers think about systems design — layers, interfaces, failure modes.

At [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), Moreau's initial mandate was straightforward: double the engineering headcount without losing the culture of rigor that made the single-pass primitives work in the first place. He introduced structured interview loops, a leveling framework, and — somewhat controversially — a "build week" onboarding process where new hires ship a small contribution to the core pipeline within their first five days. The attrition rate during onboarding is nonzero, but the engineers who make it through tend to ramp fast.

Jasper is quiet in large meetings but extremely effective in 1:1s. He has a reputation for remembering exactly where every candidate is in the pipeline and following up personaly, sometimes weeks after an initial conversation. Several of Anchor's strongest mid-career hires in late 2027 and early 2028 cite a personal email from Moreau as the thing that tipped them over.

He reports directly to the CEO and sits on the internal technical steering committee. Day to day he splits his time between recruiting, sprint planning, and architecture reveiws for the deterministic pipeline. He doesn't write much production code anymore, but he still reviews critical PRs and occasionally prototypes tooling for the build system.

Outside work, Jasper is a private person. He lives in Portland, works remotely most of the week, and flies to Anchor's offices for planning weeks and onsites.",
  "timeline": "- **2027-07-14** | First conversation with [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) leadership about the VP Engineering role; introduced through a mutual contact from his previous company.
- **2027-08-20** | Formal offer extended after a full interview loop and onsite visit with the founding engineering team.
- **2027-09-01** | Jasper Moreau officially joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as VP Engineering.
- **2027-09-18** | Introduces structured interview loops and a leveling framework for all engineering roles; some pushback from early employees but generally adopted within weeks.
- **2027-10-10** | Launches the "build week" onboarding process for new engineering hires.
- **2027-12-01** | Engineering headcount reaches 20, up from 13 when Moreau joined. Three hires attributed directly to his personal outreach.
- **2028-01-22** | Presents internal talk on team topology and scaling principles to full company; later circulated as an internal memo.
- **2028-03-05** | Leads architecture review for the next major version of the deterministic pipeline, coordinating across three sub-teams.
- **2028-05-15** | Engineering org hits 30 people. Moreau restructures into four focused pods aligned to product surfaces.
- **2028-07-02** | Hosts Anchor's first offsite engineering summit in Portland, bringing all remote and in-office engineers together for a three-day planning session."
}
